certifications_assessed.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure AI Foundations Associate 2025
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Certified Foundations Associate 2025
IT Specialist – Data Analytics (Certiport / Pearson VUE)

reasoning: Shortlist immediately — this is one of the stronger junior Python candidates you will see at this level. The highest-signal differentiator is real production AI/ML work: Shrinivas built a custom CNN-based speech enhancement system with Whisper ASR and PyTorch, and developed a two-pass candidate-job matching engine using Qdrant and Gemini 1.5 Pro at Fidelitus Corp — directly satisfying the recruiter's hard requirement of someone who has trained AI models. FastAPI depth is verified across two distinct contexts (internship microservices and personal project), and Docker experience in a 13-container production-grade environment is unusually strong for a fresher. The gaps are PostgreSQL and unit testing (PyTest/Unittest) — both are learnable quickly and neither is a dealbreaker at junior level. The internship runs through June 2026, which means he is either currently interning or just finishing — verify start availability before extending an offer. Move to interview now.
strengths: AI MODEL TRAINING (DIRECT MATCH TO RECRUITER NOTE): Built and trained a custom CNN-based audio enhancement model using PyTorch for an end-to-end speech clarity system, and applied Scikit-learn ML models for predictive sales analysis — directly satisfies the recruiter's requirement of someone who has trained AI models, which is rare at junior level.

FASTAPI + MICROSERVICES PRODUCTION DEPTH: Built a FastAPI document validation service inside a 13-container Docker microservices architecture at Fidelitus Corp, and used FastAPI again in a personal AI project — this is hands-on, real-environment FastAPI experience, not tutorial-level exposure.

COMPETITIVE CODING SIGNAL: Secured Global Rank ~1600 in TCS CodeVita Season 11 Round 2 (2025) — a high-difficulty global competition — demonstrating strong algorithmic fundamentals that directly underpin the Data Structures and Algorithms must-have for this role.
gaps: NO PYTEST/UNIT TESTING EVIDENCE: The JD explicitly requires unit testing using PyTest or Unittest, and there is zero mention of testing practices in any project or internship. For a role where writing unit tests is a stated responsibility, this is a gap that must be probed in interview — if he cannot demonstrate even basic PyTest usage, it signals a blind spot in engineering discipline.

POSTGRESQL AND JENKINS ABSENT: PostgreSQL is a listed must-have and Jenkins (CI/CD tooling) appears nowhere on the resume. While MongoDB and MySQL coverage is solid, the absence of PostgreSQL in any project — combined with no Jenkins exposure — leaves a gap in the full data/DevOps stack the JD expects even at junior level.
